title: Prometheus の 400 エラー：原因と解決策
reasons: Most factual claims are well-supported by official Prometheus documentation and related community resources.
The information regarding Prometheus API parameters, error types, and debugging methods is accurate and current.

One claim about a specific `promtool` command for client-side PromQL validation appears to be inaccurate in its precise command usage, but the general concept of client-side validation for PromQL is partially covered by other `promtool` commands.
The article provides useful and actionable advice for troubleshooting Prometheus 400 errors.

sources.title: HTTP API - Prometheus
sources.claim: Prometheus's 400 Bad Request error occurs when the format of a query or request sent by a client to Prometheus is incorrect. Invalid requests that reach the API handlers return a JSON error object and one of the following HTTP response codes: 400 Bad Request when parameters are missing or incorrect. The API response format is JSON, including 'status', 'errorType', and 'error' fields. Input timestamps for time, start, and end parameters must be provided either in RFC3339 format or as a Unix timestamp in seconds. The /api/v1/query (instant query) and /api/v1/query_range (range query) endpoints require different parameters. Series selectors and other parameters need to be URL-encoded.

sources.title: HTTP API - Prometheus
sources.claim: The HTTP API is frequently used for querying Prometheus, and errors like 400 Bad Request can be caused by PromQL syntax errors or incorrect query parameters.

sources.title: HTTP API - Prometheus
sources.claim: Prometheus cannot parse the request and cannot proceed with processing when a 400 Bad Request error occurs.

sources.title: HTTP API - Prometheus
sources.claim: The example JSON error message structure with 'status', 'errorType', and 'error' fields is consistent with the Prometheus HTTP API specification for invalid requests.

sources.title: HTTP API - Prometheus
sources.claim: The `curl` command showing a `400 Bad Request` and `time parameter has invalid format, expected Unix timestamp` is a valid example of an incorrect time parameter format.

sources.title: Troubleshoot Prometheus data source issues | Grafana documentation
sources.claim: If parentheses or quotation marks are not correctly closed in PromQL metric names or label selectors, a 400 error will occur during parsing (parse error: unexpected character or bad_data). Incorrect operators, typos in function names, and invalid label matching formats also fall into this category.

sources.title: HTTP API - Prometheus
sources.claim: The `time` parameter of the Prometheus API must be specified in Unix timestamp (integer in seconds) or RFC3339 format. Common date and time formats like `2024-01-15 10:00:00` will return a 400 error.

sources.title: HTTP API - Prometheus
sources.claim: `/api/v1/query` (instant query) and `/api/v1/query_range` (range query) require different parameters. Specifying `start` and `end` for an instant query or `time` for a range query will result in a 400 Bad Request.

sources.title: Querying basics - Prometheus
sources.claim: Prometheus dashboard (default `http://localhost:9090`)'s 'Graph' tab can be used for querying, and UI often displays syntax errors.

sources.title: Troubleshoot Prometheus data source issues | Grafana documentation
sources.claim: The Prometheus expression browser can be used to test queries directly for syntax errors.

sources.title: Feature flags - Prometheus
sources.claim: The `promtool` command provides functionalities like `check config` and `check rules` for validation, and `query` for executing queries against a server. The `promtool promql` command (experimental) is for formatting and editing PromQL.

sources.title: HTTP API - Prometheus
sources.claim: When using the HTTP API, if user input is included in query parameters, it is necessary to check whether URL encoding is correctly performed, especially when manually constructing URL strings.

sources.title: PromQL Examples - Prometheus
sources.claim: For complex PromQL expressions, referring to the official Prometheus documentation 'PromQL Examples' and comparing with standard query patterns is effective.

sources.title: Prometheus issues filtered by '400 error' - GitHub
sources.claim: Similar 400 error cases have been reported in past issues on the Prometheus GitHub repository, so searching there can be helpful.

sources.title: Are detailed logs availble in Prometheus? - Stack Overflow
sources.claim: The command `./prometheus --log.level=debug` enables debug logging for Prometheus.

sources.title: Access Prometheus queries using the Prometheus HTTP API - Thomas Suedbroecker's Blog
sources.claim: `curl -v` is a standard way to get verbose HTTP debugging output.

unsupported_claims: Prometheus 2.40 以降ではクライアント側でクエリを事前に検証できる `promtool` コマンドが提供されています。 `promtool check query 'up{job="prometheus"} / rate(http_requests_total[5m])'`
improvement_suggestions: Optional improvement: add official or primary source URLs for unsupported or weakly sourced claims.

Optional improvement: Clarify the `promtool` command for client-side PromQL validation. The `promtool check rules` command validates PromQL within rule files, and `promtool promql` (experimental) is for formatting/editing. The `promtool check query` command with the provided syntax is not standard.
Optional improvement: Review current official docs and update changed API/spec/pricing details.
Optional improvement: Add official or primary source URLs for unsupported claims.
